'''Faarlsun''' was a [[Color/Legends|yellow]]-white [[Star/Legends|star]] located in the [[Faarlsun system/Legends|Faarlsun system]],<ref name="WbC" /> a part of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space's]] [[Parthovian Cluster/Legends|Parthovian Cluster]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The two closest [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] positions around Faarlsun were occupied by the searing-[[Temperature/Legends|hot]] rock [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jun (planet)|Jun]] and the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] known as the [[Zossi Belt]]. Further out, the star was circled by two terrestrial worlds, the [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] [[Weken]] with its two [[Moon/Legends|moons]] and the habitable [[Pochi]] with three natural satellites of its own. The outer orbital positions in the Faarlsun [[Star system/Legends|system]] were occupied by the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]–type planets [[Koss]] and [[Calipp]], which had eight and twelve moons, respectively.<ref name="WbC" />

Pochi was a dry planet that was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Sentience/Legends|semi-intelligent]] [[Tulvaree]] [[Bird/Legends|avians]].<ref name="WbC" /> At some point by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Faarl]] arrived in the [[Star map/Legends|uncharted]] star system. Faarl established his base of operations on Pochi and named the system and its star after himself.<ref name="WbC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Faarlsun was mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Faarlsun system, and therefore the star Faarlsun, in grid square T-4.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
